content:
- Added Batch 3 execution notes to the task file and kept passes false.
- Ran /bin/bash .ralph/task_switch.sh as required; it updated current_tasks trackers and sent the finished-task notification.
- Next engineer should start from the next startup decision point for this docs story: re-check docs/src coverage, and if all quadrants now look sufficiently populated, run the final blind docs-only ask-k2 review before allowing passes true.

- The required commit succeeded, but the post-commit docs publish hook failed on Mermaid validation in docs/src/reference/debug-api.md.
- I am inspecting that generated reference page now and will do a follow-up fix so the branch is not left with a known docs-lint break before push.
- After the fix I need another commit and then git push.

- Fixed the Mermaid lint break in docs/src/reference/debug-api.md by replacing the invalid `/debug/verbose` node syntax with a quoted label.
- Running the docs Mermaid linter directly now to verify the post-commit hook will not fail again for this branch.
- If that passes, I need a follow-up commit for the fix and then git push.

- The docs publish hook exposed a second docs-lint policy failure: reference pages may not use fenced `json` code blocks in this repo.
- I replaced those fence languages with `text` in docs/src/reference/debug-api.md and docs/src/reference/ha-decisions.md.
- Running make docs-lint now so I can clear the hook path before the final push.

- Read .ralph/current_tasks.md and .ralph/current_tasks_done.md, plus the active task files needed to resolve priority and blocker state.
- Found no active bug tasks and no completed blockers in current_tasks_done; also found no explicit <blocked_by> tags in the active task files.
- The active failing tasks include one in-progress high-priority docs task and two later high-priority docs tasks whose descriptions depend on the docs corpus being built out first, plus several medium/low-priority code tasks. The most logical next task is the in-progress docs batch task: .ralph/tasks/story-docs-k2-expansion/01-task-run-k2-docs-loop-in-five-way-parallel-batches.md.
- Next step before quitting: write that path into .ralph/current_task.txt and stop immediately.

- Read .ralph/current_task.txt and identified active task as .ralph/tasks/story-docs-k2-expansion/01-task-run-k2-docs-loop-in-five-way-parallel-batches.md.
- Reading the task file now to determine whether it is in TO BE VERIFIED or NOW EXECUTE phase before doing any edits.
- Need to follow the task-file phase strictly and keep writing progress frequently.

- Read the active task file and confirmed it is already in NOW EXECUTE mode.
- Next step is to load only the k2-docs-loop and ask-k2 skill instructions needed for the docs-only execution/final review path.
- Need to avoid exploratory detours and follow the task-file workflow exactly.

- Ran the startup coverage check on the published docs/src corpus; counts now look materially balanced enough to attempt the final blind review gate.
- Sending a docs-only blind Diataxis review to K2 now using only the published markdown corpus under docs/src and no code/repo context.
- If K2 still flags a thin quadrant or concrete operator-significant gap, I must keep passes false and run another five-page batch instead of closing the task.

- The final blind docs-only ask-k2 review passed; it explicitly said the corpus is complete enough across all Diataxis quadrants with no obvious gaps.
- Updated the task file to record the final blind review notes, mark the remaining acceptance criteria complete, set passes true, and mark status completed.
- Next I need to run .ralph/task_switch.sh, commit all task-state changes including .ralph files, and push.
